text.title: Churn Analysis Process Checklist
text.summary: Ordered steps for establishing churn definitions, measuring baseline, segmenting causes, and setting up monitoring.

list.item: Define customer churn with explicit cancel-date and grace-period rules
Define user churn separately if product has free tier (no login in X days)
Choose measurement period (monthly is standard for SaaS)
Separate voluntary from involuntary (failed payment) churn in schema
Document definitions in every dashboard description
Calculate monthly customer churn rate and MRR churn rate
Calculate NRR (start + expansion - churn) / start
Report voluntary and involuntary churn separately
Calculate cohort-specific churn (signup quarter, plan, channel)
Benchmark vs industry standards for your segment
Identify when users churn: day 1, week 2, month 3, year-1 renewal
Compare churned vs retained user behavior (login frequency, feature usage)
Survey every churner with 6 fixed-taxonomy reasons; free text as supplemental only
Interview high-value churners (top 20% by MRR)
Analyze support ticket patterns in last 30 days before cancel
Categorize churn reasons; identify top 2-3 preventable categories
Segment churn by plan tier, signup channel, signup cohort quarter
Segment by usage level: power users, casual users, at-risk
Identify geography-specific churn patterns
Separate involuntary churn for dunning/recovery analysis
Define 4-6 behavioral signals as health score inputs
Backtest thresholds against last 90 days of actual churn data
Set alert thresholds for CS outreach (score below 40)
Recalibrate health score thresholds quarterly
Create monthly churn dashboard: customer churn, MRR churn, NRR, voluntary/involuntary split
Add cohort churn view segmented by plan, channel, quarter
Set up alerts for churn rate exceeding threshold
Generate weekly at-risk account list from health score
Track churn reasons over time to measure intervention impact
